content: Probiotics are live microorganisms that provide health benefits when consumed in adequate amounts.
Short-chain fatty acids are produced during the fermentation of prebiotics by gut bacteria and provide energy to probiotics.
Probiotics help maintain a balance of nutrients and organisms in the gut, preventing bad bacteria from causing harm.
Antibiotics can negatively impact gut flora by wiping out both good and bad bacteria.
Triclosan, found in some hand sanitizers, can also negatively affect gut flora.
Poor diet and increased inflammation can lead to a decline in gut flora health.
Probiotic supplements are equal ratios of prebiotics and probiotics, promoting a balanced gut flora.
